checks.a_analysis: I have compared the text content of the provided JSON sections with the corresponding slides in the source PDF. Section 1 accurately reflects Slide 1, Section 2 reflects Slide 2, and the closing sections (33-34) match the final slides of the deck. No invented text or fabricated sections were found in the output. The AI-generated titles and alt text are consistent with the document content and are within the allowed changes.

The contact information on Slide 34 (Web: www.ablr360.com, Call: 833-294-2257, Email: kim.casey@ablr360.com) is perfectly preserved in Section 34 of the JSON output. No other contact details are present in the provided JSON elements, and no fabricated information was identified.

The source PDF contains 34 slides. The JSON metadata reports a page count of 34, and the section numbering (1-34) corresponds directly to these pages. Although intermediate sections are omitted for token limits, the structure confirms that the conversion pipeline recognized and attempted to convert all pages in the source.
The source document consists entirely of slides with text and images; there are no tables present. The JSON output correctly identifies a table count of 0.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: Representative sections were verified for completeness. Section 33 (VoiceOver Resources) contains all 5 list items from Slide 33. Section 2 (Agenda) contains the full introductory sentence and all 3 bulleted topics. Section 34 contains all contact lines. No significant text omissions were found within the processed sections.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: The output text maintains high fidelity to the source wording. Formatting cues such as underlines for hyperlinks (e.g., in Section 33) are correctly captured in the semantic structure. No changes to the meaning of the content were observed.

checks.a_analysis: The content in the converted JSON faithfully represents the source without duplication. For instance, slide titles like 'Agenda' and 'VoiceOver Resources' are correctly mapped to section titles without being repeated in the body elements. Page numbers (e.g., '1', '34') present in the source are correctly omitted from the content elements, preventing duplication of navigational artifacts.

The heading hierarchy is consistent and logical for a slide presentation. Each slide title is used as a section title (acting as the H1/primary heading for the page context). In Section 34 ('Get in touch'), the secondary prominent text 'Thank You!' is correctly identified as a Level 2 heading (H2), maintaining a valid nested structure (H1 equivalent -> H2).
There are no tables present in the source document or the converted output (table_count: 0).

The reading order follows the logical flow of the slides. Multi-column layouts, such as on Slide 34, are linearized appropriately. The conversion correctly prioritizes the section title, followed by the body content. While the internal heading 'Thank You!' is placed before the contact details in Section 34, this is a reasonable accessibility optimization for prominent text on a slide.

Lists are correctly identified and structured. On Page 2, the three bulleted topics are converted into a single 'unordered' list with three distinct items. Similarly, Page 33's resources list is accurately reflected in the JSON structure, preserving the list's meaning and grouping.

The output correctly identifies hyperlinked text in the source. URLs on Slide 34 (Web and Email) and Slide 33 (Resources) are captured with their text content and appropriate underlining formatting. No fabricated links were found.

checks.a_analysis: The HTML output correctly converts the multi-page slide deck into a linear, single-column document. The logical progression of information (sections and headings) matches the order of the source PDF slides. While the slide-based formatting is removed in favor of a web layout, the visual organization of the primary text information is preserved.
The source PDF does not contain any formal data tables. While some slides contain screenshots of forms or lists of commands, there are no structural tables to evaluate for rendering defects.

The document is a visual instructional guide containing numerous essential screenshots of software interfaces (Acrobat, MS Word, NVDA, macOS, and iOS settings) and a QR code. In the HTML output, almost all of these meaningful images are missing or broken, appearing instead as large, solid green rectangular blocks. This is a significant failure of visual fidelity as the core instructional content is lost.
checks.b_evidence_source: Source PDF Page 5 shows a screenshot of the Microsoft Forms interface; Page 34 shows a QR code for contact information.
checks.b_evidence_output: In the HTML output, the 'Microsoft Forms' section and 'Thank You!' section contain large green blocks instead of the corresponding images.
checks.b_evidence_location: Microsoft Forms section (Slide 5) and Thank You! section (Slide 34)

checks.a_analysis: There is no evidence of visual content duplication in the HTML output. Content blocks and headings appear once in the sequence corresponding to the source slides. Logos that appear multiple times in the PDF correctly appear in their respective sections in the HTML without redundant repetition.
